economic_notes: First Nation operates Muskoday Store and Gas Bar, Muskoday Economic Development Limited, Muskoday Economic Development Authority Limited Partnership. Capital projects underway include Water Treatment Plant ($9.5M budget), Solid Waste Transfer Station, Subdivision Expansion, School Upgrades, Community Access Road, and new Store and Gas Bar construction. TLE Trust has purchased 6,650 acres to date. 40% ownership in Ironhorse Fleet Management Limited Partnership.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022), audit report dated April 22, 2023 - late filing due to COVID-19 delays. First Nation was not in compliance with loan covenant requiring financial statements within 150 days of year end, though extensions were granted. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($17,634,067) + CMHC ($1,153,327) + STC ($4,421,436) = $23,208,830 however STC may include non-government components; used ISC + CMHC + STC = $23,208,830 for total_government_transfers and adjusted OSR accordingly. Council remuneration expense was $409,965 but individual ranges not disclosed in schedule of remuneration. Contaminated site accrual of $244,800 for 2005 gas bar fuel leak. CFS Demo internally restricted surplus of $1,877,134. TLE Trust restricted investments of $4,360,043. Large surplus of $9.3M driven primarily by high ISC funding recognition from deferred revenue drawdowns and capital project funding.
